content: Primary churn causes in finance apps: (1) input friction — manual data entry feels tedious; (2) non-actionable reports — charts without guidance feel like an 'expensive calculator'; (3) rigid categories/timelines that don't match real life; (4) overwhelming onboarding that requests bank link + goal setup + budget config all at once; (5) cluttered UI amplifies financial stress. Finance apps retain only 4.5% of users at day 30. Banking apps lose 77% of users in 90 days. Decision fatigue from too many choices is a critical secondary driver.
